subject: Marshall Ave still not plowed — again
body_clean: Mayor. Third time this week Marshall Ave between 18th and 19th hasn't been touched after a snowfall. I'm 74 years old. I can't get my car out. The main roads are fine. The side streets are forgotten. Someone needs to answer for this.

subject: Marshall Ave still not plowed — again
body_clean: Mr. Kowalski, thank you for writing. I hear your frustration and I'm sorry you've had to deal with this repeatedly. I've forwarded your message to Director DiMeo and asked him to confirm that Marshall Ave between 18th and 19th is on the second-pass route and gets cleared today. I'll personally follow up to make sure. Please don't hesitate to call my office directly at 708-555-0141 if you're still blocked in by morning.

subject: Marshall Ave still not plowed — again
body_clean: Street got plowed this afternoon. Later than it should have been, but done. I'll keep watching. Thank you.

subject: Plow knocked over my mailbox on Bohland Ave
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo. I'm writing because a village plow clipped my mailbox at 714 Bohland Ave sometime Tuesday night during the snowstorm. The post is snapped and the box is in my front yard. I have photos. I understand the plows have to work fast but I need to know how I file a claim to get this replaced. My mail carrier can't even stop now. Thank you for any help.

subject: Plow knocked over my mailbox on Bohland Ave
body_clean: Ms. Sobieski, thank you for reaching out to the Mayor's office. For plow-related property damage claims, please complete the Village's Property Damage Claim form, available at Village Hall (3200 Washington Blvd) or I can email you a PDF copy. Include your photos and note the date of the incident. Claims are reviewed by the Village Attorney's office within 30 days. I'll flag your address so Public Works is aware while the claim is pending. Please reply if you'd like the PDF sent directly.

subject: Eastern Ave and 25th — ice sheet, no salt at all
body_clean: Mayor, the intersection at Eastern and 25th Avenue has been a solid sheet of ice since the freezing rain Monday. I watched two cars slide through the stop sign this morning. Nobody got hurt but it was close. I called the non-emergency line and they said to email the Mayor's office. Can someone please get some salt out there before someone gets seriously injured? This is Tuesday morning, February 11th.

subject: Eastern Ave and 25th — ice sheet, no salt at all
body_clean: Mr. Lattimore, Director DiMeo here. Eastern at 25th is on the priority intersection list. We had a truck break down overnight which set us back on the east side routes. A crew with a salt spreader is en route this morning — should be treated within the hour. I appreciate the heads-up on the vehicles sliding. We're also logging the intersection for a second pass this afternoon.

subject: Plow damage to parkway — 408 Geneva Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing to report significant damage to my parkway at 408 Geneva Ave. The snowplow came through on December 19th and pushed a wall of ice and gravel chunks onto my parkway, killing several ornamental grasses I had planted along the curb. The sod itself is now gouged about four inches deep in a six-foot stretch. I have lived here 31 years and this is the worst damage I have seen. I would appreciate a response.

subject: Giant pothole on Washington Blvd near 50th
body_clean: Hi Mayor, there's a massive pothole on Washington Blvd just west of 50th Ave, right lane heading east. I blew a tire on it last Thursday and I'm out $180 on a new one. I flagged it online but nothing's happened. It's probably 18 inches across and deep enough to swallow a hubcap. I'm not the only one — I saw two other drivers swerve around it this morning. Please get a crew out there.

subject: Giant pothole on Washington Blvd near 50th
body_clean: Mr. Webb, thanks for the specific location. My crew patched that stretch on Washington this morning — hot mix was available and we got a temporary repair in before the next cold snap. It'll need a permanent asphalt repair in the spring when temps hold above 40 consistently. On the tire damage, you'd need to file a Village property damage claim; I can tell you honestly those are rarely approved for potholes, but you're welcome to try. Contact Village Hall for the form.

subject: Rice Ave between Mannheim and 25th — no plow in 36 hours
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, my name is Josephine Mroz and I live at 2209 Rice Ave. We had the big snow Sunday night into Monday and by Tuesday afternoon my block still hadn't seen a plow. I had to work Tuesday and my neighbor helped push my car out. The snow was past my wheel wells. I know Mannheim gets done fast because the state handles it, but Rice Ave is a village street and we pay taxes too. Please send someone.

subject: Rice Ave between Mannheim and 25th — no plow in 36 hours
body_clean: Ms. Mroz, thank you so much for writing and I'm sorry about the difficult morning. You're absolutely right that Rice Ave is a village responsibility and your neighbors deserve timely service. I've already been in touch with Director DiMeo and confirmed your block will be prioritized today. We did have equipment running around the clock but the side-street second passes took longer than they should have after this storm. I appreciate your patience and I'm glad your neighbor was there to help.

subject: Plow blocked my driveway with a 3-foot ridge
body_clean: Mayor, every single storm the plow comes through Hirsch Ave and pushes a giant ridge right across the end of my driveway at 1104 Hirsch. I get it — they have to go somewhere with the snow. But that ridge is solid packed ice by the time they're done. I'm a single dad, I leave at 5:45 AM, and I spend 40 minutes every storm digging out the same two feet of packed snow that I already cleared. Is there any way to request that plow operators lift the blade slightly near driveways on residential streets?

subject: Plow blocked my driveway with a 3-foot ridge
body_clean: Mr. Gilmore, I hear you on the driveway ridge — it's one of the most common complaints we get and honestly one of the hardest to eliminate completely. Our plow geometry on a residential-width street like Hirsch means full blade contact is necessary to clear the pavement edge. I can flag your block and ask drivers to angle the discharge away from your side when conditions allow, but I can't guarantee it every pass. I'll pass the note to the crew supervisor for that route. I appreciate you explaining the situation.

subject: Pothole at Marshall and 19th is back
body_clean: Mayor. The pothole at Marshall and 19th Ave that got patched in November is open again. Bigger than before. You can see it pulling apart at the edges. They need to do it right this time, not just dump cold patch in January. I've been calling this in for two winters now.

subject: Unsalted Harvard Ave — really dangerous for school kids
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo. I want to flag that Harvard Ave between Washington Blvd and Monroe St was icy and completely unsalted on Wednesday morning, January 15th, during the time kids were walking to school. My daughter slipped and fell — she's okay, just a bruised knee, but this is a walking route to the elementary school. I called in around 7:20 AM. Salt trucks didn't come through until closer to 9. Could Harvard Ave be treated earlier on school days, especially after freezing rain overnight?

subject: Unsalted Harvard Ave — really dangerous for school kids
body_clean: Ms. Beaumont, I'm relieved to hear your daughter is okay, and I'm grateful you wrote. A child slipping on an icy school walking route is exactly the kind of situation we want to prevent. I've asked Director DiMeo to add Harvard Ave between Washington and Monroe to the early-morning salt priority list on days when we've had overnight freezing precipitation. That coordination with the school calendar is something we can and should be doing better. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.

subject: Unsalted Harvard Ave — really dangerous for school kids
body_clean: Mayor, thank you for the quick response and for taking this seriously. I noticed the street was treated by 6:45 AM on Friday — that made a real difference. I'll keep an eye on it through the rest of winter. Really do appreciate your office following up.

subject: Plow destroyed my lawn edge at 3318 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've been a Bellwood resident for 22 years at 3318 Bellwood Ave. Last Tuesday's plow run tore up about 15 feet of my lawn along the curb — the sod is completely ripped out and there's a mud trench where the grass used to be. The blade must have dropped too low. I've seen this happen to neighbors over the years but this is the first time it hit my property. I'd like someone from Public Works to come look at it before it gets covered by more snow. Is there a process for this?

subject: Plow destroyed my lawn edge at 3318 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Mr. Szymanski, Director DiMeo, Public Works. We do get parkway damage claims and there is a process — you'll want to fill out a Property Damage Claim at Village Hall. I'll also send one of my foremen to photograph the damage at 3318 Bellwood Ave this week while the evidence is visible. We typically do sod restoration in the spring once the ground is workable. Keep your own photos too. I'll have the foreman call you directly to set up a time.

subject: Frederick Ave still snow-covered — I need to get to dialysis
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, please, I need help. I live at 405 Frederick Ave and I have dialysis three times a week. It is now Wednesday afternoon and my street has not been plowed since the snow Monday night. My daughter tried to drive me this morning and got stuck half a block away. I missed my treatment. My nephrologist said missing a session is medically serious. I don't know who else to call. Please send a plow to Frederick Ave.

subject: Frederick Ave still snow-covered — I need to get to dialysis
body_clean: Ms. Reyes, I read your email immediately and I've already called Director DiMeo directly. A plow is being dispatched to Frederick Ave right now — this is a medical priority and I'm treating it as one. Please call my office at 708-555-0141 when you're ready to go to your next appointment and we will do everything possible to make sure that street is passable. I'm very sorry this happened. Your health cannot wait.

subject: Plow wake-up calls at 2 and 4 AM on our street
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I fully support snow removal and I know it has to happen at night sometimes. But the plow on Englewood Ave between 19th and 25th has been running with its backup alarm going beep beep beep repeatedly between 2 and 4 AM two nights in a row. It woke my infant both nights. Is there any way to route trucks to avoid repeated passes in front of residential blocks in the middle of the night, or is there an alternative alarm the village can look at? I'm not trying to obstruct the work.

subject: Plow wake-up calls at 2 and 4 AM on our street
body_clean: Ms. Ostrowski, I appreciate you framing this constructively. Backup alarms are federally mandated on our equipment — we can't disable or replace them on our own discretion. That said, I'll look at the Englewood route log to see if we had a driver making unnecessary back-and-forth passes rather than running the route clean. If we can tighten the routing so we do Englewood in one direction pass and move on, that would cut down the alarm exposure. No promises, but I'll look at it for the next event.
